calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-26-2026 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-12-2025 Continued To Feb-11-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-17-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

The Regents of the University of California's unopposed demurrer to complaint is sustained without leave to amend.

The complaint filed on July 31, 2025 alleges three causes of action arising from allegedly discriminatory conduct directed towards Plaintiff by her supervisors at the University of California, San Francisco (UCSF) , eventually resulting in Plaintiff's termination from her employment on March 31, 2021.

The First Cause of Action is based on retaliation in violation of Labor Code 1102.5. The complaint does not allege that Plaintiff filed a Whistleblower Claim within one year of any of the retaliatory acts she alleges. The complaint was not filed within three years of Plaintiff's termination and is therefore barred on its face by the statute of limitations. (Code of Civil Procedure 338(a)) Because these procedural hurdles appear insurmountable, the court does not address the Regents' alternate claim that the allegations of the First Cause of Action are insufficient to constitute retaliatory action as a matter of law.

The Second Cause of Action alleges retaliation in violation of the Fair Employment and Housing Act (FEMA). Prior to filing a FEMA claim against an employer, an employee must submit a timely claim to the California Civil Rights Department. The complaint alleges that Plaintiff did not submit her claim to the Civil Rights Department or the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission until April 2, 2025. Because Plaintiff did not submit her claim or file the complaint within three years of her termination, the Second Cause of Action is time-barred. The court does not reach the Regents' alternate claim that the Second Cause of Action fails to state sufficient facts to constitute a cause of action.

The Third Cause of Action alleges wrongful termination in violation of public policy. The Government Claims Act bars common law claims from being pursued against public entities such as the Regents of the University of California: "Except as otherwise provided by statute: (a) A public entity is not liable for an injury, whether such injury arises out of an act or omission of the public entity or a public employee or any other person." (Government Code 815(a); Miklosy v. Regents of the University of California (2008) 44 C. 4th 876, 899).

"Where the nature of the plaintiff's claim is clear, and under substantive law no liability exists, a court should deny leave to amend because no amendment could change the result." (City of Atascadero v. Merrill Lynch, Pierce, Fenner & Smith, Inc. (1998) 68 CA 4th 445, 459) Plaintiff did not oppose the demurrer. She has not demonstrated a reasonable possibility that the complaint's defects could be cured by amendment. (Melton v. Boustred (2010) 183 CA 4th 521, 529) Accordingly, the demurrer to each cause of action shall be sustained without leave to amend.

Proposed order submitted.

Visiting Judge: Cindee Mayfield; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CM)
